paragraphs.text: Good Day I dont even know where to start, there are so many issues piled up during 20(?) years without regulation or supervison, im a gamer since the c64 era and things have changed so much and not for the better, especially after 2015. If I have to outline a few major things i consider the mai problems, that would be 1 : the universal use of Unreal Engine 5, the engine itself might be good but what it does is allow developers without experience or mediocre understadning of making a game, to make a game. It also discoruages long term employing from companies, since everyone is using the same engine they can just swap people around whenever they want. The lack of experience to optimise or to get familiar with said engine or the actual teams who create the game, make those games feel and look like the same, while having bad performance. 2: Which lead to increased hardware requirements to just brute force our way through the bad coding. Since gaming become mainstream and investors realised there is money to be made, both software and hardware prices just going up and up. 3: paying for overpriced games, which i dont even own, i have to agree to a 3rd party eula which states it can be removed at any time at any reason. The increased video game prices doesnt even make since companies use AI as much as possible to cut costs, not to mention those segments lack of any soul or depth or creativity or culture or quality. 4: The payment processors should process my payment, and not tell me what i can or cant do with my money. 5: As meantioned above the 3rd party eual/tos shouldnt have any legal power, everything should be regulated by elected members of the government and not suits. 6: The 2 hour refund policy. Any game can hold my interest for 2 hours thats why im trying out in the first place. I cant be aware of any bugs or performance issues without playing. 7: Gamepass; what it does it just encourages companies to put out soulles products, people just rent the game for a few hours then move on, they get their money either way. Instead of; directly supporting and trusting the developers to make a quality piece of art, that i will enjoy returning to. And a lot of small issues that i dont think worth spending on energy now, the way things are now. Thank you for your time i am glad to see these things are finally being adressed, because this degeneration and corporate greed will sure not end at video games. Best of health to you
